name: French
blurb: French is spoken by ~300 million people across France, Canada, Belgium, Switzerland and much of Africa. It shares thousands of cognates with English, so you start with a hidden head start.
paretoSummary: 80% of everyday French comes from a tiny core: the ~600 most frequent words, the present tense of 4 irregular verbs (être, avoir, aller, faire) plus regular -ER verbs, noun gender with articles (le/la/un/une), the passé composé for the past, futur proche (aller + infinitive) for the future, and the ne…pas negation. Nail pronunciation early (nasal vowels, silent endings, liaison) and learn vocabulary in fixed phrases, not isolated words. Master this 20% and you can understand and be understood in most daily situations.
